name: UdpOpen
title: UdpOpen
syntax: dword UdpOpen( dword ipv4Address, dword port); // form 1
description: Depending on the address type, the function creates either an IPv4 or IPv6 UDP socket for use in connectionless, datagramm-oriented communications. All parameters may be zero. If the port parameter is non-zero, the socket is implicitly bound to the given port. Otherwise the socket can be bound later on with IpBind or it will be automatically bound to a free port with UdpSendTo.
If an address is given but the port is zero, no implicit bind is performed. Please use IpBind after opening the socket in this case.

parameters.name: ipv4Address
parameters.description: The numerical local IPv4 address to be used with the socket.

return: INVALID_SOCKET (~0): The function failed. Call IpGetLastError to get a more specific error code.
